page: Amazon Web Services c8ib.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance belonging to the c8ib server family. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor, this instance features 16 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU allocation operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor to minimize virtualization overhead and maximize resource allocation to the guest operating system. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 17 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

The c8ib.4xlarge is designed for compute-heavy workloads that require high CPU clock speeds but do not demand large memory footprints or local storage. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-efficient choice for raw processing tasks compared to memory-optimized instances, though the lack of local storage requires architectural planning for disk-intensive applications. Suitable workloads include batch processing, high-performance web serving, distributed analytics, and compute-intensive scientific modeling.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor to minimize virtualization overhead, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. This hardware profile is engineered for compute-intensive workloads that require high clock speeds and dedicated CPU resources without the need for massive memory arrays or local disk storage. The instance is suited for tasks such as batch processing, distributed analytics, and high-performance web serving.
